findings.description: XSS vulnerability: Page names are not escaped in renderHTML. The title attribute and inner text are inserted without HTML encoding, allowing potential XSS if page names contain malicious HTML/JavaScript.
findings.suggestedFix: Use the existing escapeHtml function: mergeAttributes({ ..., 'data-page': escapeHtml(page), title: `Open page: ${escapeHtml(page)}` }), and escape the inner text. Alternatively, use text() helper or set textContent.

findings.description: Unused parameter: The existingPages parameter in handleOpenWikiLink is never used. The code checks the file system directly instead of using the passed page list.
findings.suggestedFix: Either use the existingPages parameter to check if the page exists before doing file system stat, or remove the parameter entirely to avoid confusion.

findings.description: sanitizeFileName can return empty string: If the input is all invalid characters (e.g., '<<<>>>'), the sanitization would produce an empty string, leading to '.html' file creation.
findings.suggestedFix: Add validation: if (sanitizedName.length === 0) { vscode.window.showWarningMessage('Invalid page name'); return; }

findings.description: Inconsistent HTML escaping: The escapeHtml function exists but renderHTML doesn't use it, while the input rule handler does use it for fallback. This creates an inconsistency in how page names are handled.
findings.suggestedFix: Use escapeHtml consistently in renderHTML or use a dedicated text node for the link content.

findings.description: Duplicate tests: Tests are embedded at the bottom of WikiLink.ts and duplicated in PageCreation.test.ts. This creates maintenance burden and potential confusion.
findings.suggestedFix: Remove the embedded tests from WikiLink.ts and keep only PageCreation.test.ts.

findings.description: Empty page names are allowed: The extension accepts empty page names which could lead to unexpected behavior.
findings.suggestedFix: Add validation in the command handler or input rule to reject empty page names.

summary: The page-creation feature fulfills VAL-BACKLINKS-004 by implementing wiki link click handling that opens existing pages or prompts users to create new ones. However, there are two blocking security issues (XSS vulnerability in renderHTML and unused existingPages parameter) that should be addressed before merging.
